title: Advertising Manager
keyDrivers: This role sits at the top of a function where AI is compressing the middle. The core task — planning and preparing advertising material — shows 30.8% automation across 2,700+ observed conversations, meaning the execution work that managers oversee is being increasingly automated. The role scores low because it combines AI-exposed output work with a declining market for traditional advertising management: demand for these roles was already being squeezed by programmatic advertising before AI added a second wave of pressure.
taskIntro: Not all tasks are affected equally. Knowing which ones AI handles well, and which still need a human, is how to focus skill-building.

risks.body: Campaign trafficking, media buying coordination, and performance monitoring — the execution layer of advertising management — are being absorbed by AI-powered programmatic platforms, eliminating the manual coordination that justified mid-level manager roles. eMarketer reports that AI is actively reshaping how programmatic ad platforms operate, automating the optimization and trafficking work that traditionally required dedicated advertising managers [eMarketer, 2025]. BLS projects a -2% decline in advertising and promotions manager employment through 2033 — one of the few white-collar management occupations with a net projected loss — driven by platform consolidation and AI absorbing the media-buying workflow [U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, Sep 2024].
risks.statLabel: BLS projected employment decline for advertising managers through 2033
risks.statSourceTitle: Occupational Outlook Handbook: Advertising, Promotions, and Marketing Managers

opportunities.body: As AI floods marketing channels with templated content, audience demand for strategically differentiated, human-directed campaigns is rising — creating a durable premium for managers who can set creative direction and brand voice rather than execute production tasks. HubSpot’s 2026 State of Marketing survey of 1,500+ global marketers found that 62.7% believe the industry needs more unique, human-centered content to compete with AI-generated material [HubSpot, 2026]. Advertising and promotions managers who redirect their time from trafficking and media-buying coordination toward brand strategy, cross-channel narrative coherence, and AI-output oversight are best positioned to capture this demand shift.
opportunities.statLabel: of marketers say human-centered content is needed to compete with AI

howToAdapt.alreadyIn: Build fluency in AI-assisted campaign tools now so the work you oversee is produced faster and at lower cost — this matters because budget pressure is ongoing in this sector. Over six months, shift your value proposition toward the parts of the role that AI handles worst: client strategy, cross-functional coordination, and the judgment calls about creative direction that require context no tool has [Kantar, Sep 2024]. Managers who can operate with leaner teams by using AI effectively are more valuable than those who manage large teams producing output that AI could generate [eMarketer, Apr 2024].
howToAdapt.thinkingOf: With only 2,100 annual job openings and negative projected growth, this is a competitive field to enter. Build a portfolio that demonstrates measurable campaign outcomes — not just campaign management experience — since employers are increasingly focused on ROI rather than headcount [HubSpot, Mar 2024]. A path through digital marketing or content strategy roles provides a stronger foundation than traditional advertising agency work, which is facing the steeper structural decline [eMarketer, Apr 2024].

quotes.quote: The advertising managers who are navigating this environment well are those who have redefined their value around strategy and client relationships, while using AI tools to dramatically reduce the cost and time of production work their teams previously spent most of their time on.
quotes.attribution: Future of Jobs Report 2025, World Economic Forum

quotes.quote: Marketing and advertising is one of the functions where generative AI creates the most economic value — which means the practitioners who adapt are those who shift from managing content production to managing AI-assisted systems and directing strategic decisions.
quotes.attribution: The economic potential of generative AI, McKinsey Global Institute

quotes.quote: Programmatic platforms now manage 91% of U.S. digital display ad spending automatically, but the remaining strategic decisions about brand positioning, creative direction, and cross-channel budget allocation are where advertising managers justify their roles.
quotes.attribution: US Programmatic Digital Display Ad Spending 2024, eMarketer

quotes.quote: The advertising managers most in demand combine creative judgment with data literacy — they can evaluate both the AI-generated campaign variants and the brand implications of running them.

taskData.full: Plan and prepare advertising and promotional material to increase sales of products or services, working with customers, company officials, sales departments, and advertising agencies.
Plan and execute advertising policies and strategies for organizations.
Confer with department heads or staff to discuss topics such as contracts, selection of advertising media, or product to be advertised.
Devise or evaluate methods and procedures for collecting data, such as surveys, opinion polls, and questionnaires.
Monitor and analyze sales promotion results to determine cost effectiveness of promotion campaigns.
Coordinate activities of departments, such as sales, graphic arts, media, finance, and research.
Inspect layouts and advertising copy, and edit scripts, audio, video, and other promotional material for adherence to specifications.
Analyze the effectiveness of marketing tactics or channels.
Read trade journals and professional literature to stay informed on trends, innovations, and changes that affect media planning.
Manage sales team, including setting goals, providing incentives, and evaluating employee performance.

careerCluster.title: Marketing Specialist
careerCluster.fit: Entry-level research and communications role that builds the analytical and market knowledge foundation.
steps: Build a portfolio of research summaries and data visualizations
Learn SQL and a BI tool (Tableau or Power BI)
Develop subject matter expertise in a specific industry vertical

careerCluster.title: Search Marketing Strategist
careerCluster.fit: Digital marketing specialist role; search/SEO skills complement content strategy and brand management.
steps: Earn Google Analytics and Google Ads certifications
Build a portfolio showing SEO-driven content performance
Learn paid media fundamentals via Meta Blueprint

careerCluster.title: Public Relations Specialist
careerCluster.fit: Moving to PR Specialist is a step down from Advertising Manager in seniority, but a lateral shift in function — the focus moves from paid and promotional media to earned media and stakeholder communication.
steps: Build media pitching and press release experience
Develop media contact relationships in a target industry
Learn crisis communications and spokesperson coaching

careerCluster.title: Copywriter
careerCluster.fit: Ad & Promotions Managers often oversee writers and can step back into producing their own work — it is a step down in management scope but a move toward creative autonomy and individual craft.
steps: Build an independent bylined portfolio
Develop copywriting clients or publication relationships
Write across commercial formats (ad copy, content, editorial)

careerCluster.title: Editor
careerCluster.fit: Ad Managers who specialize in the content quality side of their role can step into editing — it is a step down in management scope, shifting from campaign oversight to editorial judgment and content standards.
steps: Build a substantive editing portfolio (3+ publications)
Study AP Style and editorial decision-making
Develop experience managing a content calendar or publication

careerCluster.title: Marketing Manager
careerCluster.fit: Advertising & Promotions Manager is a natural step toward Marketing Manager — it is a step up that expands scope from campaign execution to full marketing strategy, brand management, and digital channel integration.
steps: Build experience managing digital channel budgets
Add brand and product marketing to campaign work
Manage a cross-functional marketing team

careerCluster.title: Advertising Sales Agent
careerCluster.fit: Advertising Sales Agents who develop platform expertise and campaign analytics experience can transition into the Advertising & Promotions Manager role — the sales background provides client relationship skills, while the new role adds creative direction and campaign budget ownership.
steps: Build Google Ads and Meta Ads campaign management experience
Develop campaign analytics and reporting skills
Move from selling ad products to planning and executing campaigns

emergingCareers.title: AI Campaign Strategist
emergingCareers.description: Directs AI-assisted campaign planning and creative development across paid, owned, and earned channels, using generative tools to accelerate production while applying human judgment to brief quality, channel mix, and brand fit. Evaluates AI-generated creative concepts and media plans before they enter market.
emergingCareers.core_tools: Claude API, Google Performance Max, Meta Advantage+

stat.text: Marketing teams using AI report 20-30% faster content origination and production, shifting advertising manager value from overseeing production to evaluating AI output and making final creative calls.
stat.sourceTitle: Executive Perspectives: The Future of Marketing with GenAI

emergingCareers.search_query: "AI campaign strategist" OR "campaign AI strategy" advertising jobs 2025
emergingCareers.fit: Campaign planning, creative briefing, and channel strategy carry directly; the shift is from managing production teams to directing AI tools that handle production, with human judgment concentrated on strategy and evaluation.
steps: Run a documented AI-assisted campaign from brief to launch using Claude for copy and Performance Max for media
Complete Google AI Essentials and Meta Blueprint certifications for AI campaign tools
Build a creative evaluation rubric that scores AI-generated concepts against brand and strategy criteria

emergingCareers.title: Programmatic Advertising Specialist
emergingCareers.description: Manages AI-powered programmatic ad buying, audience targeting, and creative optimization across display, video, and connected TV. Configures automated bidding strategies, interprets performance signals, and adjusts campaign structure when AI optimization underperforms.
emergingCareers.core_tools: Google DV360, The Trade Desk, LiveRamp

stat.text: 66% of marketing professionals now use AI in their roles, with programmatic ad optimization and audience targeting among the most rapidly automated functions in advertising operations.
stat.sourceTitle: HubSpot AI Trends for Marketers Report

emergingCareers.fit: Advertising channel knowledge, audience strategy, and performance measurement carry directly; the new skill is managing AI bidding systems and diagnosing when automated optimization diverges from campaign goals.
steps: Complete The Trade Desk Edge Academy certification
Run a programmatic campaign on DV360 with documented audience segmentation and optimization decisions
Study LiveRamp data clean room documentation to understand privacy-safe audience targeting

emergingCareers.title: Marketing Technology Strategist
emergingCareers.description: Evaluates, implements, and governs the AI-powered marketing technology stack for an advertising or marketing organization. Owns vendor selection, integration architecture, and the workflow systems that connect AI tools across campaign management, creative production, and analytics.
emergingCareers.core_tools: Salesforce Marketing Cloud, HubSpot AI, Claude API

stat.text: Marketing technology spending stabilized at 24% of total marketing budgets in 2025, with AI-powered tools driving a structural shift in how advertising organizations are staffed and operated.
stat.sourceTitle: Executive Perspectives: The Future of Marketing with GenAI

emergingCareers.search_query: "marketing technology strategist" OR "martech" AI stack jobs 2025
emergingCareers.fit: Campaign operations knowledge and cross-functional coordination carry directly; the new scope is owning the technology architecture decisions that determine how AI tools are selected, integrated, and governed across the advertising function.
steps: Complete Salesforce Marketing Cloud certification
Conduct a marketing tech stack audit and produce a consolidation recommendation for a current or past employer
Build familiarity with CDP architecture via Segment or Tealium documentation

emergingCareers.title: Creative AI Producer
emergingCareers.description: Oversees the production of AI-generated advertising creative at scale, managing the workflow from brief to approved asset across video, display, and social formats. Sets quality standards for AI-generated creative, manages human review processes, and measures creative performance.
emergingCareers.core_tools: Runway ML, Adobe Firefly, Claude API

stat.text: Companies using GenAI for advertising creative report 60% reduction in time-to-market for campaign assets and 25-50% lower spending on agency production, creating demand for in-house AI creative operations roles.
stat.sourceTitle: Executive Perspectives: The Future of Marketing with GenAI

emergingCareers.search_query: "AI creative producer" OR "AI advertising creative" production jobs 2025
emergingCareers.fit: Creative briefing, production oversight, and brand quality control carry directly; the new context is managing AI tools that produce creative assets rather than directing human creative teams.
steps: Complete Adobe Firefly and Runway ML tutorials to build hands-on AI creative production skills
Produce a multi-format AI creative campaign from brief to final assets and document the production workflow
Build a creative quality evaluation rubric covering brand fit, message clarity, and technical specs

sources.name: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics
sources.title: Occupational Outlook Handbook: Advertising, Promotions, and Marketing Managers

sources.name: eMarketer
sources.title: How programmatic ad platforms are evolving with AI features

sources.name: Lightcast
